title: The Liouville conformal field theory quantum zipper
speaker: Morris Ang
abstract: Sheffield showed that conformally welding a γ-Liouville quantum gravity (LQG) surface to itself gives a Schramm-Loewner evolution (SLE) curve with parameter κ=γ2 as the interface, and Duplantier-Miller-Sheffield proved similar stories for κ=16/γ2 for γ-LQG surfaces with boundaries decorated by looptrees of disks or by continuum random trees. We study these dynamics for LQG surfaces coming from Liouville conformal field theory (LCFT). At stopping times depending only on the curve, we give an explicit description of the surface and curve in terms of LCFT and SLE.

proof_stages.label: Classical LQG, SLE, and quantum zipper input
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines LQG geometry from the Gaussian free field, recalls SLE and reverse SLE, and states the existing quantum zipper stationarity/independence theorem for simple and self-touching SLE regimes.

proof_stages.label: Space-filling quantum zipper via mating of trees
proof_stages.summary: Using the Duplantier-Miller-Sheffield mating-of-trees encoding by correlated Brownian continuum random trees, the speaker states the analogous zipper theorem for kappa at least eight, where the zipped interface is space-filling SLE and the modulo-constant GFF law is stationary.

proof_stages.label: Passage from modulo-constant LQG fields to LCFT fields
proof_stages.summary: After constructing Liouville fields and correlation functions by integrating over the additive constant, the talk observes that adding a Lebesgue-distributed constant converts the modulo-constant zipper into an LCFT quantum zipper, with insertions transported by reverse SLE and weighted by conformal derivatives.

proof_stages.label: Boundary BPZ equations from zipper martingales
proof_stages.summary: For a degenerate boundary insertion and a special relation between left and right boundary cosmological constants, the LCFT zipper yields SLE martingales whose Itô drift is exactly the boundary BPZ differential operator, proving the BPZ equations for both degenerate weights.

proof_stages.label: Role in the boundary LCFT bootstrap program
proof_stages.summary: The proven boundary BPZ equations are positioned as key inputs for computing disk boundary structure constants and for a broader conformal-bootstrap program aimed at solving LCFT on surfaces with boundary.

proof_stages.label: Whole-plane SLE reversibility problem
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines reversibility, contrasts the non-reversibility of chordal SLE for kappa greater than eight with known whole-plane results for smaller kappa, and states the goal of proving whole-plane SLE reversibility in the space-filling regime.

proof_stages.label: Radial LCFT mating-of-trees encoding
proof_stages.summary: The LCFT zipper is used to obtain a radial mating-of-trees theorem for an LCFT disk decorated by radial space-filling SLE, encoding the surface and curve by a boundary-length Brownian motion stopped at zero and an independent left-right imbalance Brownian motion.

proof_stages.label: Sphere limit and reversibility deduction
proof_stages.summary: Taking the boundary length to zero gives an LQG sphere with whole-plane SLE encoded by a Brownian excursion plus an independent Brownian motion, and the symmetry of both the marked sphere and the time-reversed encoding implies reversibility of whole-plane SLE for kappa at least eight.
